comments.author: @LisaZapotockyVelez-Norcia
comments.text: There was a part of the beginning of this video showing that Antarctica was connected to some othrr places long ago and there was no ice or anything.  So, people could have been living there then, right?

comments.author: @Bernard-j6g
comments.text: Great video - fine work - what an incredible location.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you for watching.

comments.author: @sara505sings
comments.text: Why why why? Why must these videos all be made with obnoxious unending background music? Can we please have one video with just a calm voice, speaking without frenetic music in the background.

replies.author: @sara505sings
replies.text: @WhereonEarth4K thank you, and there can be some silence too you know. There’s a balance.😊

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Appreciate the direct feedback — we'll take another look at the music balance so it doesn't overwhelm the narration on this one.

comments.author: @asullivan4047
comments.text: Excellent video coverage enabling viewers 👀 to better understand what the orator is describing 🤗. Thanks for the tour 😉

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you for watching.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you for watching — really glad you enjoyed it.

comments.author: @micahmiller234
comments.text: Prior comment is right on.  The Aircraft Squadron I was in 62 years ago as a Para-Rescue  Corpsman landed various craft ferried from Rhode Island at Williams Field airstrip carved from the Ross Ice Shelf.  I made 9 jumps there, , we used helicopters, easy out.  During a whiteout once we had to use a SnowCat to rescue a pilot down near McMurdo.  Guided by radar, potential crevasses, a long traverse. We were highly alert.  Pilot uninjured.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: That's an extraordinary account — a whiteout rescue by SnowCat near McMurdo sounds like its own documentary. Thank you for the detail and for the service. Did that kind of alertness ever become second nature out there?

comments.author: @timwilliams9875
comments.text: Weather hasn't changed since Gods creation in the Antartica. Good documentary

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you for watching — really glad it landed with you.

comments.author: @SilentWanderYT-TV
comments.text: This is a beautifully crafted documentary! The storytelling around the human, animal, and scientific resilience in such an extreme environment is incredibly captivating. The visuals paired with the history of the Heroic Age and the modern reality of life on the ice make for a truly immersive experience. Thank you for sharing such an insightful look at the most isolated place on Earth!

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you — really glad the human and scientific side came through alongside the scale of the place. Which thirty seconds of it would you choose to live inside?

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you — really appreciate you saying that.

comments.author: @spidermight8054
comments.text: De-salinating ocean water is easier or more efficient than melting ice?

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: At most Antarctic stations, melting snow or ice actually wins — desalination needs a lot of energy-hungry infrastructure to ship in and maintain, while ice is sitting right outside the door. It just takes heat, which stations already have to generate anyway.

comments.author: @aaajp3
comments.text: I LIKED THE VID BUT TO BE 100 - WOULD IT KILL YOU TO ADD A MAP OR 2 - REALLY - IS IT THATY HARD.??

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Fair ask — a map or two would help ground the geography for people who aren't already familiar with the region. Noted for future episodes.

comments.author: @danielmason3501
comments.text: the volume is very, very low , please increase the volume on the videos thanks.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you for the feedback regarding the volume; we appreciate you tuning in. 🌿

comments.author: @oldrango883
comments.text: Best food on earth has been proven to be in the USA! 2026 World Cup reactions have all proven it. People around the world were shocked and brought to tears at how good our food is.

comments.author: @trueglobe66
comments.text: Just as I imagined, life here is indeed very harsh.

replies.author: @David_W2
replies.text: So much so that anyone who gets to work in Antarctica often will only get to do so for a few months each year before going home.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you for watching — glad the film matched what you expected to find.

comments.author: @SherryPeterson-e2c
comments.text: Why a scene with pine trees?

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Good question — let us know which timestamp and we'll take a closer look at whether that footage matches the region.

replies.author: @leonardrice2830
replies.text: Probably to reflect on the transition from lush warm climate to frigid one, my thought.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: They probably have some kind of message. But I can't figure out why they did that 😅

comments.author: @Aventurex-686
comments.text: Antarctica looks like Earth accidentally left the freezer door open for a few million years. Yet people still manage to build communities, do science, and keep life moving. That's more impressive than anything in my toolbox. Which part of life there surprised you the most?

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: "Left the freezer door open for a few million years" — that might be the most accurate description of Antarctica ever written. Thank you for watching. 🌿

comments.author: @balancespirit4706
comments.text: An ad every 70 seconds, how should we watch? Not every has ads free YouTube. Very annoying to watch so couldn’t watch until the end

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Sorry that got in the way of the watch — ad frequency for non-Premium viewers is largely set by YouTube's own placement system rather than something we hand-tune per video, but we'll take a look at our settings on this one.

replies.author: @kenappel6149
replies.text: If you are on a touch screen, cell or tablet then drag the movie as close to the end as possible and then let it play out. Some other shows will pop up, pull them down then hit the rotating arrow. Movie or whatever will then play add free except maybe right at the end an ad will pop up, figure this out and enjoy

replies.author: @oldrango883
replies.text: If you watch enough YouTube then go premium. You can afford it!

replies.author: @Ebenezerscrooge24
replies.text: That's how they push people towards premium, no ads
